cli/create.title: actio create
cli/create.description: Create a new project with the full Actio sidecar and ACTIO.md entry file.

cli/doctor.title: actio doctor
cli/doctor.description: Run the same checks as actio validate but always exit 0. Use it for a quick health check without failing scripts or CI.

cli/init.title: actio init
cli/init.description: Add the Actio sidecar to an existing repository. Does not overwrite existing files.

cli/mcp.title: actio mcp
cli/mcp.description: Start a minimal MCP-compatible (Model Context Protocol) server over stdio. Exposes Actio resources so AI tools can list and read sidecar files.

cli/validate.title: actio validate
cli/validate.description: Check that the Actio sidecar is present, valid, and consistent. Fails with a non-zero exit code if there are issues.

cli/version.title: actio version
cli/version.description: Print the version number of Actio.

concepts/domains.title: Domains
concepts/domains.description: Domains in Actio are named areas of your system (e.g. connectors, api, billing). Each domain has its own architecture, interfaces, and patterns so agents get scoped context.

concepts/index-yaml.title: index.yaml
concepts/index-yaml.description: actio/index.yaml is the context router. It tells agents which files to read for which domain, rule, or task.

concepts/rules-and-tasks.title: Rules and tasks
concepts/rules-and-tasks.description: Actio separates rules (what must always be true) from tasks (how to do a specific thing). Both are referenced from index.yaml.

concepts/sidecar.title: The Actio sidecar
concepts/sidecar.description: The sidecar is a directory named actio/ at your project root. It holds all context that AI agents are supposed to read before changing code.

getting-started/create-project.title: Create a project
getting-started/create-project.description: Use actio create to scaffold a new project with the full Actio sidecar.

getting-started/installation.title: Installation
getting-started/installation.description: Install the Actio CLI to create projects, validate sidecars, and run the MCP server.

getting-started/quick-start.title: Quick Start
getting-started/quick-start.description: Get Actio running in under a minute.

guides/mcp-integration.title: MCP integration
guides/mcp-integration.description: Actio can expose its context to AI tools via a minimal MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. Run actio mcp and connect your tool over stdio.

guides/plugins.title: Plugins
guides/plugins.description: Plugins extend Actio validation with extra checks (e.g. "this file must exist"). They are YAML files under actio/plugins/.

guides/schema-validation.title: Schema validation
guides/schema-validation.description: Actio validates actio/index.yaml against a schema and checks referential integrity so the sidecar stays consistent.

guides/use-cases.title: Use cases
guides/use-cases.description: Actio is use-case agnostic. The same sidecar layout and index work for many kinds of projects. Below are common patterns.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: Actio (AI Context Toolkit) is an AI-sidecar framework that gives AI coding agents (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) structured context so they follow your architecture and rules instead of guessing.
